Returns the newly created User object with authentication token. Side effects: marks invitation as used, creates user record, and initializes user profile.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about required inputs (invitation ID, credentials), response content (User object, token), and state changes (invitation marked used, user created). Enhanced version provides complete information for AI agents to understand prerequisites, expected request format, response structure, and resulting system state changes.

data.current_description: Check if an object exists. Requires 'object:read' ACL.
data.description_suggestion: Checks if an object exists without retrieving its content. Expects object ID as a path parameter. Requires 'object:read' ACL. Returns HTTP 200 if object exists, 404 if not found. Useful for lightweight existence verification before full object retrieval.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about response codes and use case. Enhanced version specifies HTTP status codes (200/404), clarifies the lightweight nature of HEAD request, and explains when an AI agent should use this operation versus getObject for better interpretability.

data.current_description: Delete an object by ID. Requires 'object:delete' ACL.
